title: Lab Aide
description:
**CancerCare Manitoba** is a provincial agency which provides clinical and academic services and leadership for cancer control and the treatment of blood disorders. Our shared vision of a **world free of cancer** drives every member of our team. CancerCare Manitoba is the perfect place to thrive if you're seeking a fulfilling career in an organization that is committed to its employees' well\-being and professional development.

  


**Competition \# 061\-26\-761020301**


**Posting End Date:** March 18, 2026


**City:** Winnipeg


**Work Location:** MacCharles


**Department:** Paul Albrechtsen Research Institute


**Union:** CUPE FS


**Hiring Status:** Permanent


**Anticipated Start Date:** ASAP


**FTE:** 0\.50


**Anticipated Shift:** Days


**Daily Hours Worked:** 7\.75


**Annual Base Hours:** 2015


**Salary:** $19\.947 /$20\.530 /$21\.137 /$21\.755 /$22\.394 /$23\.051

  


**Job Summary:**

  



The Lab Aide is responsible for cleaning a wide variety of laboratory glassware, plasticware, equipment for researchers within Paul Albrechtesen Research Institute. Incumbent is responsible for providing essential services including sterilizing solutions and dryware used in experimental work. In addition, the incumbent maintains the cleanliness of the Wash\-up Room, lab sink areas and assorted joint\-use areas.

  


**Qualifications:**

  


**Education**


* Successful completion of Grade 12 education or equivalent

  


**Experience**


* Experience working in a laboratory environment is preferred
  


An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

  


**Skills and Abilities**


* Ability to work both independently and as a member of a multi\-disciplinary team
* Ability to work under minimal supervision
* Demonstrated attention to detail and ability to follow details instructions

  


**Physical Demands and Working Conditions**


* Ability to perform physical activities such as; bending, twisting, and heavy lifting and carrying up to 50 lbs
* Ability to stand and move around for extended periods of time.
* Incumbent is exposed frequently to high temperatures, humidity and working with unpleasant odours.

  


**Other**


* Must have satisfactory employment record
* Must be legally able to work in Canada
* Ability to speak French

  



Interested and qualified applicants are invited to submit a detailed and current resume, plus a cover letter demonstrating how you meet the above qualifications and the above competition number.

  


***This position is subject to a Criminal Record Check, including Vulnerable Sector Search, an Adult Abuse Registry Check and a Child Abuse Registry Check. The successful candidate will be responsible for any service charges incurred.***

  


*CancerCare Manitoba is committed to developing inclusive, barrier\-free recruitment processes and work environment. If you are contacted by CancerCare Manitoba regarding a job opportunity or testing, please advise if accommodation measures must be taken to enable you to apply and be assessed in a fair and equitable manner.*

  



We thank all that apply and advise that only those applicants selected for further consideration will be contacted.

title: Research Assistant - Tech 3 (AESES)
description:
Department of Medical Microbiology \& Inf. Diseases
Rady Faculty of Health Sciences  

Position number: 37542
Date posted: March 10, 2026
Advertised until: March 17, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Research Assistant \- Tech 3 (AESES)

New Project Full\-Time (Grant Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: No
  

Work schedule: 35 hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:00 am \- 8:00 pm), hours will be flexible with occasional evenings and weekends
  

Salary: $23\.13 to $33\.29 per hour ($42,096\.60 to $60,587\.80 per annum)
  

Appointment Dates April 6, 2026 to April 5, 2027
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Leah Buermeyer \- Leah.Buermeyer@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------



**Responsibilities:** **RESEARCH**


* Process and store research samples (blood \[PBMC, serum, plasma, whole blood], urine, nasal swabs, saliva, fecal wipes, among other human samples).
* Keep record of the laboratory research samples received and stored.
* Request quotes from vendors.
* Place reagents and materials orders through EPIC.
* Keep an inventory of the wet laboratory materials, reagents, chemicals and equipment.
* Update and renew biosafety permit of the principal investigators.
* Process samples for research experiments as assigned.
* Prepare reports and summaries of the activities on a regular basis.
* Document research activities in notebooks, laboratory notebooks, and online, keeping the highest standards of research ethics and confidentiality.
* Work collaboratively with the research team members.
* Other duties as assigned.
* In\-person work is required.


**TRAINING \& COMMUNICATION**


* Participate in job training by:
	+ Attending a 1\-2 day training session that will be held in person.
	+ Complete all mandatory research training required by the UM and by our research team within the first two\-three weeks of the starting date (Tri Council Policy Statement 2: Course on Research Ethics, and refresher sessions held virtually, University of Manitoba PHIA).
	+ Engaging in self\-learning before and after each training phase, including to attend a cultural sensitivity workshop to further enhance and develop cultural awareness and cultural sensitivity skills.
	+ Attend team meetings and other training activities organized by our team

* Communicate with the team members.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.
  




**Key responsibilities**
------------------------



**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING REQUIRED:**


* Bachelor's Degree in a related study area i.e sciences, biomedical sciences, or related disciplines, with demonstrated training and experience working in wet laboratories and processing human samples, including laboratory analysis techniques.

OR
* Completion of a Community College course in a related discipline with one years \= directly\-related experience.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* Previous work or research experience processing human samples \[blood \[PBMC, serum, plasma, whole blood], urine, nasal swabs, saliva, fecal wipes, among other human samples] is an asset.
* People from traditionally marginalized or silenced communities are encouraged to apply, including women, racialized and Indigenous persons; men who have sex with men, lesbian, bi, queer, two\-spirit and transgendered people; and other individuals from groups who have been historically under\-represented in health research.
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* Demonstrated training and experience working in wet laboratories processing human samples is required.
* Demonstrated training in biosafety is required.
* Request quotes are required.
* Process and store research samples (blood \[PBMC, serum, plasma, whole blood], urine, nasal swabs, saliva, fecal wipes, among other human samples) is required.
* Keep record of the laboratory research samples received and stored is required.
* Request quotes from vendors is required.
* Place reagents and materials orders through EPIC is required.
* Keep an inventory of the wet laboratory materials, reagents, chemicals and equipment is required.
* Update and renew biosafety permit of the principal investigators are required.
* Process samples for research experiments as assigned are required.
* Prepare reports and summaries of the activities on a regular basis is required.
* Document research activities in notebooks, laboratory notebooks, and online, keeping the highest standards of research ethics and confidentiality is required.
* Work collaboratively with the research team members is required.
* In\-person work is required.E
* Excellent communication skills with a clear understanding of teamwork and respecting a diverse research team. It is expected that mutual respect, cultural awareness and culturally appropriate research methods will form the basis of the professional working relationship is required.
* Excellent organizational skills, time management is required.
* Excellent writing skills are required.
* Self\-directed and able to prioritize effectively to meet deadlines is required.
* Detail\-oriented with strong critical thinking, organization and record\-keeping skills is required.
* Ability to be collaborative and work in team\-based environments. Our laboratory has multiple collaborations with several organizations, national and international, with a strong commitment to equity, diversity, and inclusion is required.
* A satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.


 **OTHER JOB\-RELATED QUALIFICATIONS:**


* Passionate about learning.
* Passionate about creating positive change for people experiencing homelessness, people who use drugs and people living with HIV, and people diagnosed with sexually transmitted and blood\-borne infections.
**Additional information**
--------------------------

  



The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).



If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact UM.Accommodation@umanitoba.ca or 204\-474\-7195\. Please note this contact information is for accommodation reasons only.



Application materials, including letters of reference, will be handled in accordance with the protection of privacy provisions of "The Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act" (Manitoba). Please note that curriculum vitae will be provided to participating members of the search process.

title: Residential Coordinator- Inclusion Selkirk
description:
Job description:

**Overall Responsibility:**

The Residential Coordinator is responsible for the overall day\-to\-day management of the designated residential program areas, meeting the mission, vision, and values of Inclusion Selkirk. Ensuring person centered, outcome oriented services for individuals is a significant responsibility of this role. The position is responsible for guiding and developing staff teams, evaluating their performance and dealing with all employee relations and disciplinary issues. This position is actively involved in program planning, administration, and evaluation. This position supports the Team Leaders through on call situations and/ or crises

**Specific Responsibilities:**

Person Centered Outcomes:

* Initiate the gathering of information needed from people we serve and their support networks to gain an understanding of strengths, needs, and goals.
* Develop support plans enlisting the persons support team to monitor service delivery and make recommendations for changes.
* Utilizing support plans, other plans and planning tools to assist the team in achieving required supports and desired outcomes.

**Human Resource Management:**

* Identify staffing needs to management, assist with training new staff as required
* Responsible for providing direction and guidance to staff teams, resolving conflicts and employee issues, evaluating performance, providing progressive discipline where necessary, and identifying staffing and/or training needs.
* Conduct all disciplinary meetings with staff members and the union, if requested. Ensure focus is on correcting the issue and building capacity within the staff member and their team.
* Provide recommendation on disciplinary actions to the Director of Services ; uses progressive discipline

**Employee Development:**

* Provide onsite supervision to the team with a focus on role modelling the supports required with an emphasis on teaching, monitoring and coaching to develop skills within the staff team.
* Provide formal and informal feedback to employees to recognize successes, develop goals for improvement and monitor progress is overall development.

**Administration:**

* Responsible for administrative responsibilities such as ensuring the buildings, grounds, and equipment are maintained properly.
* Auditing payroll entries and monitoring program expenditures.

**Documentation:**

* Ensure completion and secure storage of records and documentation in accordance with agency policies and procedures, Residential Care Licensing and the CLdS requirements
* Review, process and provide follow up on Incident reports, medical appointments etc and ensure that issues are moved up to Director as needed

**Policy and Procedures:**

* Position is responsible for communicating policies and procedures to team leaders and staff and making recommendations if changes are required
* Position is responsible to ensure team is compliant in following policy and procedures and addressing any staff concerns with non\-compliance

**Financial Responsibilities:**

* Monitor financial matters including the departments budget and individuals personal funds.
* Monitor for the completion of items such as social allowance annual reviews, income tax, and SDM submissions. Report any suspected mismanagement of funds as per guidelines.
* Participate in the completion and annual review of financial care plans and oversee monthly auditing and review processes

**Proposal Development:**

* Identify need for additional support and/ or specialized services and identify to appropriate department heads.
* Position may be required to complete funding proposals.

**Program Evaluation:**

* Evaluate programs by maintaining regular communications with individuals, staff, families, CSW’s employees and ensure that services are working towards achieving their outcomes.

**Advocacy:**

* Promotes the concept of inclusion and full community participation.
* Support each person to seek out and achieve valued roles in the community and to develop relationships.
* Ensure the person served has a voice that is heard, and that they are provided with tools and resources needed to exercise their rights.

**Safety:**

* Ensure that residents and staff live and work in a safe environment.
* Ensure compliance with all safety rules and regulations.
* Audit that all food, medication, equipment and properties are properly stored, maintained, and used safely and that all safety plans are up to date and are followed.

**Health and Medical:**

* Audit to ensure individuals health and medical needs are accurately addressed in a timely manner and that all medical follow up is completed
* Ensure medication is properly stored and administered according to CLdS and Licensing standards.
* Recognize and report concerns of abuse and neglect

**Communication and Relationships:**

* Build and maintain positive working relationships with all stakeholders.
* Communicate necessary information to ensure holistic supports are present.
* Ensure interactions demonstrate value for all people at all times.
* May be required to participate in difficult conversations and must do so with respect to all parties concerned.
* Make sure all practices meet legal and ethical standards related to confidentiality and professional conduct.
* Ensure that the team is providing opportunities for relationships with families, neighbours, and community and to foster the relationships important to that person.

**Qualifications:**

* A related Bachelor's Degree or Disability and Community Support Program Diploma is preferred
* Minimum of 3 years’ experience in a Senior Management role
* Excellent working knowledge of Windows, MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Office 360
* Demonstrated effective leadership skills
* Strong and effective interpersonal skills
* Exceptional communication skills (verbal and written)
* Strong organizational skills
* Effective problem\-solving skills
* Ability to prioritize
* Valid class 5 driver’s license
* Must be a self\-starter who is able to multi\-task and be organized
* Must be creative and innovative
* Demonstrated ability to advocate and promote self\-advocacy

**Schedule:**

\-Monday to Friday

\-On call

\-Weekends as needed

**We appreciate all candidates and their interest in Inclusion Selkirk, due to high volumes of applicants, we will only be contacting those selected for an interview.**

Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent

Pay: From $23\.30 per hour

Benefits:

* Casual dress
* Company pension
* Dental care
* Disability insurance
* Extended health care
* Flexible schedule
* Life insurance
* On\-site parking
* Paid time off
* Vision care

Ability to commute/relocate:

* Selkirk, MB: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred)

Experience:

* Working with persons with developmental disabilities: 3 years (preferred)
* Management or Supervisor: 1 year (preferred)

Licence/Certification:

* Class 5F License (required)

Location:

* Selkirk, MB (preferred)

Work Location: In person

Landscape Construction Project Manager
description:
**Job Summary**

The Landscape Project Manager is a pivotal leadership role responsible for the seamless planning, execution, and delivery of high\-quality landscape construction projects. This position bridges the gap between the design vision and the physical reality, ensuring that projects are completed on time, within budget, and to the highest standards of safety and client satisfaction. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with a strong background in construction management, horticulture, and team leadership.

**Key Responsibilities**

**1\. Project Planning \& Pre\-Construction**

* **Contract Review \& Handoff:** Lead the transition of projects from the sales/design phase to operations. Review plans, specifications, and contracts to ensure scope clarity and identify potential challenges (e.g., utility conflicts, grading issues).
* **Budgeting \& Procurement:** Develop detailed project budgets, material take\-offs, and procurement schedules. Source and order plants, hardscape materials (pavers, natural stone), irrigation components, and specialty items.
* **Scheduling:** Create comprehensive project timelines using project management software (e.g., MS Project, BuilderTrend, CoConstruct), sequencing trades for maximum efficiency and minimal disruption.

**2\. On\-Site Management \& Execution**

* **Team Leadership:** Directly supervise and coordinate field crews, including foremen and subcontractors. Communicate daily and weekly goals, ensuring clear understanding of design intent and quality standards.
* **Quality Control:** Conduct regular site inspections to ensure all work adheres to design plans, industry best practices, and company quality standards (e.g., proper plant installation, precise hardscape laying, correct grading for drainage).
* **Safety Compliance:** Enforce strict adherence to OSHA standards and company safety protocols. Conduct regular safety meetings and site audits to maintain a zero\-incident workplace.
* **Equipment \& Logistics:** Coordinate the delivery of materials and ensure crews have the necessary equipment and tools to perform their work efficiently.

**3\. Client \& Stakeholder Communication**

* **Client Liaison:** Serve as the primary point of contact for clients during the construction phase. Provide regular progress updates, manage expectations, and professionally address any questions or concerns.
* **Problem Solving:** Proactively identify potential issues (design conflicts, weather delays, material shortages) and develop effective solutions to keep the project moving forward.
* **Permitting \& Inspections:** Coordinate with local municipalities to secure necessary permits and schedule required inspections to ensure code compliance.

**4\. Financial Management \& Administration**

* **Cost Control:** Continuously monitor job costs against the budget, tracking labor, materials, and equipment usage. Approve timesheets and vendor invoices.
* **Change Order Management:** Identify and document any out\-of\-scope work, prepare detailed change orders for client approval, and manage the associated budget adjustments.
* **Project Closeout:** Conduct final walk\-throughs with clients, create punch lists, and ensure all work is completed to satisfaction. Oversee the final billing and archive all project documentation (as\-builts, warranties, maintenance schedules).

**Qualifications \& Requirements**

* **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Landscape Architecture, Horticulture, Construction Management, or a related field preferred. Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered.
* **Experience:** Minimum of 3\-5 years of experience in landscape construction project management, with a proven track record of managing complex projects from start to finish.
* **Technical Knowledge:**
* Deep understanding of landscape construction techniques (hardscape, softscape, drainage, irrigation, low\-voltage lighting).
* Proficiency in reading and interpreting architectural and engineering blueprints.
* Strong mathematical skills for calculating material quantities, area, and volume.
* **Certifications/Licenses:**
* Valid driver’s license with a clean driving record.
* Pesticide Applicator’s License (preferred, or ability to obtain).
* Certified Landscape Professional (CLP) or similar industry certification (a plus).

**Skills \& Competencies**

* **Leadership:** Ability to motivate, direct, and develop field crews, fostering a positive and productive team environment.
* **Communication:** Excellent verbal and written communication skills; ability to articulate complex technical information to clients, designers, and crew members.
* **Organization:** Exceptional attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities, deadlines, and budgets simultaneously.
* **Tech\-Savvy:** Proficiency with project management software, Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook), and experience with CRM systems.
* **Problem\-Solving:** Resourceful and calm under pressure, with the ability to make sound decisions quickly in a dynamic outdoor environment.

**Working Conditions \& Physical Requirements**

* **Environment:** Work is performed both in an office setting and outdoors at active construction sites. Outdoor work involves exposure to various weather conditions (heat, cold, rain, humidity), dirt, dust, and noise.
* **Physical:** Ability to walk the job site frequently, stand for long periods, and navigate uneven terrain. Must be able to lift up to 50 pounds occasionally.
* **Schedule:** Typically full\-time, Monday through Friday, with occasional weekend work required to meet project deadlines or respond to client needs.

Pay: $55,000\.00\-$70,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Extended health care
* Paid time off
* RRSP match

Work Location: In person

title: Project Coordinator/Estimator
description:
Penn\-co is founded on the principles of inspiring and empowering communities to build a brighter future. We strive to collaborate with community leaders to achieve their goals, advocate for their needs, and provide them with the tools and resources necessary to drive positive change.

  

Are you looking for an exciting and challenging career path? Are you passionate about construction and building on your future? Opportunities for professional growth and development along with a collaborative and community\-focused culture

  

Penn\-co Construction is currently seeking a hybrid Project Coordinator\-Estimator to join our team at our Steinbach office. This role will consist of a hybrid of quantity take\-off work along with administrative and technical support for the life cycle of a project to ensure smooth execution.

  

Penn\-co offers a competitive salary and an excellent benefits package. If you are looking to join a leader in the construction industry and bring projects to life, then this may be the perfect fit for you.

  


**Primary Responsibilities**


* Perform material take\-offs \& quantity calculations
* Review drawings, specifications, and contract documents to understand project scope and requirements.
* Liaison with various project teams with respect to quantity takeoffs and production rates

  


**Secondary Responsibilities**


* Process and track change orders, RFIs, and shop drawings.
* Support on\-site coordination of personnel and subcontractors, ensuring clear communication and proper documentation.
* Maintain organized and accurate project documentation in accordance with contractual requirements and company standards.
* Solicit, evaluate, and analyze pricing from subcontractors and suppliers to ensure accuracy, competitiveness, and compliance with project requirements.
* Participate in health, safety, quality, and environmental programs.
* Assist with project close\-out activities, including inspections, deficiency management, warranty coordination, and demobilization.
* Collaborate with the Estimating Team, Site Superintendent, and Project Manager to ensure successful delivery of all bid and project requirements.

  


**Qualifications**


* Diploma in Civil Engineering Technology (CET), Construction Management, Civil Engineering, or a combination of relevant education and practical experience.
* Experience in the construction industry with a strong understanding of estimating principles and project coordination.
* Strong analytical, organizational, and problem\-solving skills with a high level of attention to detail.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office, and Bluebeam.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast\-paced environment.
* Strong communication skills with the ability to be assertive and effectively resolve site and subcontractor issues.
* Valid driver’s license.
* Willingness to work flexible hours as required.
* Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.

  



Penn\-co has been in business for 65 years! Learn more about our projects and company culture at **www.penn\-co.com**. Empowering today for a better tomorrow.

  

Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Estimator/Project Manager
description:
**Overview**  
We are seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented Estimator/Project Manager to lead our estimation team in bidding structural steel and miscellaneous metals in the construction industry. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of 5 years estimating steel structures, a minimum of 3 years as a project manager, strong negotiation skills, with a proven track record of delivering accurate and competitive bids. This role offers an opportunity to build processes that oversee complex projects, collaborate with cross\-functional teams, and contribute to the growth of our company’s portfolio. The Estimator/Project Manager will have the opportunity to pursue jobs from the estimation phase through to completion of the project and will play a critical role in ensuring our projects are accurately priced and aligned with client expectations.

**Duties**

* Prepare detailed quantity takeoff calculations and cost estimates for projects, including labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor and time (general conditions) requirements.
* Prepare detailed cost estimates using industry\-standard tools such as Tekla and Bluebeam
* Collaborate with Operation Managers, clients, and subcontractors to gather accurate pricing data
* Analyze project plans, specifications, and drawings to determine scope of work and resource requirements
* Develop competitive pricing strategies while maintaining profitability margins
* Oversee the negotiation process with vendors and subcontractors to secure favorable terms
* Review and validate all estimates for accuracy, completeness, and compliance with company standards
* Monitor market trends, material costs, and labor rates to ensure estimates reflect current conditions
* Assist in the development of bid proposals and presentations for clients
* Work closely with the production Manager and installation team to ensure projects are being completed on time and on budget as estimated
* Manage and bill change orders

**Requirements**

* Minimum 5 years Proven experience in construction estimating, with a strong understanding of construction processes and project management principles
* Proficiency in Bluebeam, Tekla, and other estimating or project management software
* Excellent negotiation skills to secure competitive pricing from vendors and subcontractors
* Strong analytical skills with attention to detail for accurate cost analysis and risk assessment
* Ability to lead a team effectively while managing multiple projects simultaneously
* Knowledge of construction industry standards, safety regulations, and building codes
* Effective communication skills for coordinating with internal teams and external stakeholders
* Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, or related field preferred but not mandatory
* Experience in a fabrication and/or production welding shop

This position offers a dynamic work environment where strategic thinking, leadership, and technical expertise are highly valued. The successful candidate will be instrumental in driving profitable growth through precise estimation practices.

Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent

Pay: $60,000\.00\-$90,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Casual dress
* Company events
* Dental care
* Extended health care
* Flexible schedule
* On\-site parking
* Paid time off
* RRSP match
* Vision care

Work Location: In person

title: Construction Project Coordinator
description:
Construction Project Coordinator  


Matix Lumber Inc. is a family and locally owned and operated professional building supply centre that provides for all your building needs. We are a building supply and project centre providing project providing contractors and do\-it yourself builders with the tools, materials, design, and construction services to support projects of any scale.  


Matix Lumber services all sectors of the building industry and offer a comprehensive selection of quality lumber and building materials manufactured by North America’s leading producers. We provide contractors, avid builders DIYers, and community leaders the tools, construction service, and design expertise to support residential and commercial projects – from start to finish.  


We believe in building collaborative, mutually beneficial relationships with everyone we work with and have been able to achieve this through our industry experience, sound project execution, and visionary thinking.  


Matix Lumber Inc. is looking for a full\-time Project Coordinator to join our growing team. We are looking for a motivated professional with on site construction experience and strong knowledge of building materials, who can provide high\-level customer service to our clients.  


The Project Coordinator supports the Project Manager(s) with planning, executing, and delivering projects on time, within budget, and according to specifications. You will work closely with trades, subcontractors, suppliers, and consultants throughout each project.  


Consider applying for this incredible opportunity!  


The Project Coordinator will:  


Assists with preparation and execution of overall work plan and schedule.  
Organizes and maintains project site files and documents.  
Manages electronic document databases and file sharing platforms.  
Maintains accurate documents for the project.  
Reviews subcontractor/supplier progress applications and payment certificates.  
Liaises with architects, engineers, subcontractors, and suppliers on contract and change notices, design revisions, RFIs, RFQs, etc.  
Prepare and participate in meetings with project team, architects, consultants, and subcontractors.  
Prepares and distributes accurate meeting minutes in a timely manner.  
Assists with preparing final turnover documentation for the client.  
Coordinate deficiencies in a timely manner.  
Maintains project awareness to keep Project Manager and team members informed of status and potential issues.  
Provides technical and administrative support as required by the project team.  
Other duties as assigned.  


The Project Coordinator will have:  


3–5 years of experience in project coordination, preferably within construction.  
Onsite experience coordinating and working directly with trades and subcontractors (required).  
Ability to read and interpret building specifications, blueprints, and as built.  
Strong mathematical and analytical skills for job cost reports and estimating support.  
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, etc.).  
Effective communications skills (oral, written, listening).  
Understanding of basic construction tools, equipment, and materials for each project.  
Strong client service and public interaction skills.  
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a team environment.  
Customer focused mindset with strong problem solving skills.  
The ability to be receptive to change and can quickly adapt.  
Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to stay focused in an open office environment.  
Ability to provide excellent internal support and follow up with team members as needed.  
Strong knowledge of building materials and construction, with a willingness to learn and grow.  


Our employees enjoy a full range of health benefits and other Company perks including benefits, store discount, tuition reimbursement, and much more.  


If you are interested in this exciting opportunity with Matix Lumber, apply today with your resume and cover letter including salary expectations to hrcareers@matixlumber.com.
